{"step_by_step_thinking": "The feed forward mechanism is a type of anticipatory control that prepares the body for a predicted change in the environment. Among the given options, salivation on smelling food (option C) is an example of a feed forward mechanism because it anticipates the need for saliva when food is about to be eaten. The other options, such as feeling thirsty in hot weather (A), shivering in cold (B), and increase in blood pressure in supine posture (D), are all reactive mechanisms that occur in response to a current change in the environment, not a predicted one.", "answer_choice": "C"}
